question.label: Team connection
question.name: How connected do you feel to the rest of the team right now?
question.description: Probes isolation or drift without naming it; one of the earliest signals of disengagement.

answer: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
note: She names a felt shift since the reorg while still trying to hold stability for the team, signaling mild disconnection and strain.

question.label: Reorg shift
question.name: You said 'the shift since the reorg' — what feels most different now?
question.description: Drills into the specific change she just named so the manager understands what shifted.

answer: I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
note: She is pulling back in leadership meetings while uncertain where her voice has leverage — strong engagement drop, with an off-signature clarity issue also visible.

question.label: Voice leverage
question.name: Where does your voice feel least useful right now?
question.description: Drills into her 'voice has most leverage' comment to locate the specific forum or decision space she is pulling back from.

answer: Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
note: She disclosed fatigue from constant context switching and that she has been holding it in, which is a strong negative engagement signal; also reveals wellbeing strain worth probing next.

question.label: Where things stand
question.name: Before we get into specifics, what feels most worth talking through today?
question.description: Safe opener that lets Elena set the agenda without priming a problem.

answer: I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.
note: She names a specific drop in groundedness without saying anything is broken, which signals reduced clarity about where she stands.

question.label: Less grounded
question.name: When you say 'less grounded' — what feels less solid day to day?
question.description: Clarifies what 'less grounded' means in her actual workday before moving on.

answer: I have been protecting my team from noise, which means I am carrying more ambiguity myself.
note: She is absorbing ambiguity to shield her team, which signals both reduced day-to-day clarity and a mild wellbeing strain.

question.label: Carrying ambiguity
question.name: Carrying more ambiguity yourself — where is that showing up most right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific place the ambiguity is landing before moving to support.

answer: I am not fully clear yet on how success is being measured in this new setup.
note: She names unclear success measures in the new setup, a direct mild negative on clarity and a likely growth-limiting ambiguity.

question.label: Support gap
question.name: What support would actually take friction out of the next two weeks?
question.description: Moves from concern to concrete support Elena can use immediately.

answer: What would help most is clearer prioritization and a little more air cover in cross-team decisions.
note: Names two specific absences — clearer prioritization and more air cover in cross-team decisions — so this is a deficiency-as-request; current support is not reducing friction.

question.label: Next two weeks
question.name: What would make the next two weeks steadier for you?
question.description: Designated closer that lands on immediate action and support.

answer: I want to re-engage more visibly, but I need a steadier footing first.
note: She wants to re-engage but says she needs steadier footing first, signalling mild instability and unresolved clarity about what would let her step back in.

question.label: Priority ranking
question.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?
question.description: Forces a real priority ranking. Vague answers are a clarity signal.

answer: I would start by naming one cross-team decision where clearer air cover would help me re-engage.
note: She could not rank work directly and instead named a missing condition—clearer air cover on a cross-team decision—as what would enable re-engagement, signaling strong clarity friction and mild disengagement.
